        • [ - ] PunterBloke - member since 01-Dec-24

          Amorim said this: "It's a dream to be here and I want to continue here and I want to fight for this. But the problem is now what makes me suffer is to lose games, not to lose my job. You fear to lose your job when you have to pay the bills and I don't have that feeling." I have that feeling, cuz I need to pay my bills. But Man U keep losing,then I dunno how to pay them. Lol!

          39 weeks ago

        • [ - ] PunterBloke - member since 01-Dec-24

          Once a player leaves Man U,he starts scoring,why?. Look at Anthony(Betis),Garnacho(Chelsea),Rashford(Barça),Höjlund(Napoli). It must be something going bad within the team,it needs to be fixed. Some players need to leave,Kirkzee,Kesko,goalkeeper. Did I miss anyone else? Lol!

          39 weeks ago
           +2 Reply Report Sign in or join us to rate comments! Sign In || Join Us
        • [ - ] PunterBloke - member since 01-Dec-24

          Last chance for Amorim,win this game or get sacked. The owners are watching him closely now,he can't keep shaking his head every time Bruno misses a penalty. Lol!

          39 weeks ago
           +1 Reply Report Sign in or join us to rate comments! Sign In || Join Us
        • [ - ] koziol.mutant - member since 03-Oct-14

          Man Ud are the best team in EPL in expected goals. They miss too many chances and expose themselves to big chances when chasing the game. Also, Bruno has already missed 2 penalties this season in awful fashion. As Amorim said, they need the right balance, which is obvious. Casemiro is still a classy midfielder. I think that Sunderland is like a public bet also, so 2-0 would not surprise me.

          39 weeks ago
           +1 Reply Report Sign in or join us to rate comments! Sign In || Join Us
